chapters.title: Helping others should be a way of life for everyone
chapters.text:
Millions of people, and many children among them, roam the streets with nothing to lean on. Most come from violent homes. Beyond the begging, the streets offer a strange comfort, a break from the chaos at home.

One act of help can change a life. Sometimes that help is what keeps someone going, especially when they have lost faith in human warmth. Laura’s friendship with Maurice started on a street corner, but it grew into something larger, and that meeting shaped his life for good.

Laura Schroff writes from her own experience and leaves nothing out. The honesty in her actions and the steady way she kept showing up for Maurice offers a clear example for anyone who wants to do the same.

We have to learn to light a candle in someone’s darkest moment. That is what gives our own lives the deepest meaning.

Alex Treniowski worked with Laura to shape the prose with care. For anyone unsure about how to help others or how to trust the pull of a strange coincidence, this book comes highly recommended.

chapters.title: Coincidence can be an invisible thread that ties strangers together
chapters.text:
Laura had decided to take a walk after a heavy rain when she ran into a small boy named Maurice on a New York street. Maurice was eleven and a beggar, and he asked Laura if she had spare change because he was hungry. Laura passed him by the way she always did, but a few steps later she stopped, turned, and went back to him.

She could not explain the impulse. She chose to call it an invisible thread of fate. She took Maurice to McDonald’s for lunch that day, and the meal became a daily ritual over the next 150 days. The new rhythm puzzled Laura, because a stream of questions ran through her mind.

Maurice was wary of Laura and could not believe an adult would care about him. Laura was unsure about the boy too. She later learned that Maurice had come from a violent and abusive home. His father, also called Morris, was in a gang, used crack, drank hard, and was married to another gang member. The father would beat Maurice and his two sisters along with their mother.

A violent life warps a person. It shows up in behavior and pushes people into acts they would never pick otherwise.

While they sat in McDonald’s, Maurice had a small knife in his pocket, but Laura had no idea. She was reading him through her own lens. Her work as a sales rep for USA Today did not impress him, since he had no schooling and no clear dreams for the future.

chapters.title: Too much excitement during good moments can lead to accidents
chapters.text:
Laura could not stop turning Maurice over in her mind, and the situation echoed her own hunt for a foothold in the working world. She had finished high school set on becoming a stewardess so travel would come easily. Her first post was a secretarial seat at an insurance firm, and from there a clerical role opened up at Icelandic Airlines. A typing test tripped her up twice before the office manager slipped her into a receptionist slot.

On her final day at the insurance firm, joy hijacked her hands at the wheel. The crash left her unmarked except for a swollen blue eye.

We have to keep good news from getting the better of us. Over excitement can push us into reckless moves.

Her Icelandic Airlines run was brief, and she moved on. A small classified caught her eye. It said, "Sell Advertising Space for Twice Weekly Travel Trade Publication." She rang for an interview even though she had no experience and no college degree. She won a job with Travel Agent magazine after arriving ahead of time with nine fresh stitches across her finger. Her energy and commitment won over the HR manager.

Small acts of kindness build trust with anyone. Be kind to everyone.

Within days of their first sidewalk meeting, Maurice had already written Laura off. He was used to being alone and could not picture anyone caring. What he did not know was that Laura was nearly desperate to learn more about him and find ways to help.

As Laura mulled Maurice over, a quiet voice inside questioned her motives. She realized that helping him lifted her own mood, and she started to wonder whether she wanted to help because she cared or because the act fed her. She needed to sort out her intentions, so she went looking for Maurice again.

Three days after they first met, Laura found Maurice and offered to buy him lunch again. He agreed at once, and they went to McDonald’s and shared a good meal. When they finished, Laura suggested making it a routine and meeting up for lunch every Monday. Maurice agreed. Laura’s friends and family were supportive at first, until she invited Maurice for a meal at her house. Most of her friends thought she had crossed a line, but Laura pressed on and invited him over. Maurice was thrilled, and when he finished eating, Laura handed him a ticket to a Mets game so they could watch together.

A city count from 2015 logged 4,827 homeless people in New York, with 2,487 in shelters and 2,340 in mother and children crisis centers.

chapters.title: Society’s definition of a good mother can be cruel and warped
chapters.text:
Laura’s childhood carried mixed feelings because her home mixed love and war. Her parents practiced love in a real way, even when violence crept in around the edges. Her father was a bartender, and her mother was a waitress. When she saw the joy on Maurice’s face, she wondered what pulled boys to baseball. Her answer came when she thought back to her younger brother Frank and how he obsessed over his mitt, cap, and bat.

Laura realized that baseball gave Frank an escape the way other things give other people an escape. For Maurice, the game was a break from his troubles. To get Maurice to the game, Laura needed a permission slip signed by his mother, so she went to his home to get the signature. Maurice lived at the Bryant Hotel, a rundown building full of trash and stink. When Laura arrived, his mother was too drunk to sign, so his grandmother signed instead.

Laura’s great grandmother was a woman who suffered under society’s wrong idea of what a good mother is. She was married to a man named Pasquale, and their life together ran well until Pasquale was drafted into the army, leaving Maria and six children on their own.

Pasquale’s departure shows how powerless women were during the war years. Things got rough for Maria and the children, and they had to scavenge for food just to survive. Pasquale visited once in a while, and on one visit Maria got pregnant again. Sensing what was coming, she went to the barracks and demanded her husband be released so he could take care of his family. The officer in charge turned her down and offered to keep Pasquale from being sent to the front lines to raise his odds of survival. Maria went home, unaware that the officer had lied, and Pasquale would still be drafted. A few weeks later, she learned that Pasquale had been killed in the war. The government stepped in and declared Maria unfit to care for all her children. Two of the seven children were taken from her. Her youngest daughter had followed her on a trip to visit Maria’s sick mother when she fell into a well while playing nearby. The government returned and took the remaining children. The damage echoed through Laura’s family until it started to shape her parents.

Generational wounds can disturb the mind, especially in women.

To protect the generations ahead, we have to watch over the mental health of women.

chapters.title: Education stays one of the most powerful tools for a bright future
chapters.text:
As time passed, Laura noticed that Maurice often went without food for days between their Monday meetings, so she decided to leave a meal for him at the door of her building so he could grab it on his way to school.

Maurice was happy with the setup, but he asked for something that nearly moved Laura to tears. He asked her to pack the meals in brown paper bags. When she asked why, he said that kids who brought meals in brown paper were kids whose parents cared about them. His schoolwork did not give Laura much to celebrate. He got bad grades, and he was either absent or late to class.

Maurice’s teacher, Miss House, showed him warmth, and together with Laura’s help, they persuaded Maurice to get to class on time and lift his grades.

Love, compassion, and care are three forces that shape a child’s life. When they are real, we can change one child at a time.

Maurice’s first trip outside New York became the spark for his future success. For the first time in his life, Maurice stepped out of the city when Laura took him to meet her sister’s family on Long Island. Annette had three children, and Maurice was surprised by how big and orderly their house was. Annette’s kids were close to his age, and they bonded quickly and had a great time. One of the biggest surprises for Maurice was the large dining table. He was so used to eating in a one room apartment he shared with twelve other people that he had never imagined a whole room and a big table set aside for meals. When they drove home, a happy Maurice promised Laura that he would get his own big table one day and share meals with his own family.

Dreams matter, and dreams come true, as Laura Schroff has put it.

Thanksgiving arrived, and instead of going to her sister’s house as usual, Laura hosted her sister and invited Maurice to join them. The plan worked, and it was one of the best holidays for everyone. The day also taught Laura something important. At her niece’s recital at the local church, a little girl burst into tears while she was on stage, and Laura feared her father would hit her. To her surprise, the girl’s father knelt down and soothed her so she could continue. Laura had expected anger based on her own past, but none came. The moment reminded her that parenting styles vary and not every father is violent.

chapters.title: There will be moments when you must choose between your life and someone else’s well being
chapters.text:
Laura and Maurice’s friendship kept growing, and it stayed strong for a long time until Laura met Michael, the man she would fall in love with. Her relationship with Michael forced a choice between her marriage and her friendship with Maurice. Michael grew so uneasy about the closeness between Laura and Maurice that he barred Maurice from visiting Laura or coming to their home.

Laura felt torn because she had promised Maurice she would never leave him alone. The situation created a rift between Laura and her husband, and the strain grew worse because Michael did not want to have children with her. Unable to bear it, Laura ended the marriage and told herself she would find peace in supporting Maurice instead. To Laura, Maurice was the child she never had, and she decided she would enjoy watching him grow up.

The best way to fix a mistake is to own it and take steps to keep it from happening again.

While Laura dealt with Michael, Maurice was also growing up fast. He got a girl pregnant before long, and a baby came into his life. Laura was deeply disappointed because she felt Maurice had moved too soon and risked what he had been building. He was still young to be a father. Her disappointment cut Maurice, and he felt the weight of it for himself and for her. One thing he understood was that he could not stay under Laura’s care forever. To grow, he had to be on his own and make his own way.

Without learning from the first child, Maurice and his partner had a second, which made the grind harder. He met with Laura and, for the first time, asked her to help raise money for his family. He hid the second child because he feared her reaction. When Laura raised the money, Maurice left and was out of sight until his mother died three years later. During the time away, he worked odd jobs and struggled on the streets to provide for his family. When he finally returned for his mother’s funeral, he reached out to Laura and told her she was his mother now. Even though she had worried herself sick, Laura was relieved to hear from him and accepted the role without hesitation.

chapters.title: The biggest reward for helping someone is watching them succeed
chapters.text:
Both Laura and Maurice took away deep lessons from their unusual friendship. For Laura’s fiftieth birthday, she threw a big party with close to ninety guests. Among the crowd were Maurice, his wife, and their five kids. Early in the evening, Maurice stood up, walked to the podium, and took the microphone. Everyone in the room knew the story and waited for his words. When he began, his voice carried the weight of gratitude.

Maurice told the room that meeting Laura was the moment that saved his life, and that she was an angel sent from above. Laura was moved to tears and felt grateful for the day they met. She believed an invisible thread had pulled them together.

Maurice had started a construction firm and had a lovely apartment with a beautiful family of his own. True to the promise he had made to Laura, he had bought his family a big dining table, which felt like a mission accomplished.

We should always help the people within our reach to help.

Helping others gives us a sense of purpose and lets us feel that we matter to the growth of the people around us.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Every person deserves a chance at a good life, but not everyone is lucky enough to cross paths with someone willing to help. Maurice’s future took a sharp turn when he met a stranger on the street, and Laura’s selfless attitude toward him shows what compassion can do.

It is always good to help people when you can, because even when you do not do it for yourself, you get a deep sense of fulfillment from playing a part in someone’s life.

Life calls for grit, focus, and determination, and even when mistakes show up, there is always the choice to move on and look for a fix.

If we have no peace, it is because we have forgotten that we belong to each other, as Mother Teresa once said.

Never be too reluctant to help a stranger when you have the capacity. Be selfless in what you do, and stay with people until they can stand on their own.
